CHAPTER ONE

The Spotlight

Brand Activation
APR 2026 · CHINA
LIMITED COLLABORATION · APR 2026

Sttoke meets LESS.
A study in quiet luxury.

In April we joined hands with LESS — one of China's premier fashion houses — to bring Sttoke into a curated retail context. The collaboration unfolded across five flagship multi-brand boutiques, presenting our cups not as drinkware but as design objects, displayed alongside ready-to-wear from one of the country's most respected design teams.

Partner
LESS
Stores
5
Format
In-store
Boutique footprint
5stores
Premium multi-brand boutiques across Tier-1 cities
Activation type
2levers
Curated in-store display + direct sell-through

Premium fashion buyers · category-adjacent reach
Hero SKUs
3colourways
Matcha · Mousse Taupe · Lavender — plus Moomin co-brand keychains
In-Store Activation Sttoke × Moomin co-brand · LESS retail floor
Editorial Display Hero colourways with NEW ARRIVAL ready-to-wear
Outdoor Series Catalogue · five-sense story
Gift-with-Purchase Moomin keychain · scan-and-share campaign
The Detail Matcha cup & Moomin keychain on stone+moss styling
i. Why It Matters

Premium fashion retail is one of the few channels where a premium-priced insulated cup reads as a complementary lifestyle object rather than a kitchen utility. Placing Sttoke alongside LESS ready-to-wear borrows the boutique's curatorial credibility — every cup on a marble plinth signals "design object", not "drinkware."

The collaboration also unlocks an audience we cannot buy through paid media: LESS shoppers are category-adjacent — premium lifestyle, design-led — already pre-qualified on price tolerance, and shop in physical stores where impulse purchases of accessories convert at meaningfully higher rates than online discovery.

The Moomin scan-and-share keychain mechanic adds a UGC layer: every photographed display becomes Xiaohongshu content carrying both the LESS and Sttoke brand markers. Earned reach, no media spend.

CHAPTER TWO

Melbourne 8

Hometown Launch
20 APR → 31 MAY 2026
LIVE · MELBOURNE

Buy a cup.
Taste the city.

The new 8oz Commuter launches with Melbourne 8 — a hometown campaign across eight independent cafés. Buy the cup, receive a Coffee Pass, taste a free coffee at each. A product launch dressed as a citywide invitation.

Cafés
8
Hero SKU
8oz
Window
6 weeks
Mechanic
Coffee Pass
i.
The Map
Eight cafés pinned across Melbourne — Carlton, Fitzroy, CBD, Southbank. The map is the route, and the route is the campaign.
ii.
The Pass
Buy the 8oz Commuter, receive a Coffee Pass. Visit a participating café, present the pass, redeem a complimentary coffee. Stamped on each visit.
iii.
The Café
Sttoke is displayed on the counter, beside the brew bar, in plain view at point-of-purchase. The cup becomes part of the café's aesthetic — not a sponsor placement.
ON DISPLAY · MELBOURNE CBD
ON THE COUNTER · BREW BAR
CURATED RETAIL · BESIDE PREMIUM GIFTING
i. The Story

Most cup launches happen on a website. We launched ours on a map. The 8oz Commuter — Sttoke's smallest, most portable size — was never meant to be discovered through a hero shot on Instagram. It was meant to be discovered the way Melbourne discovers its best things: in a café, in someone else's hand, on a counter you walked past on the way to somewhere else.

The mechanic is simple: buy the cup, get a pass, redeem a free coffee at each of the eight cafés. The complexity is in what the mechanic produces — a six-week game played across the city, where the prize is exploration and the receipt is a stamped Coffee Pass nobody throws away.

Melbourne 8 is not a marketing campaign attached to a product. It is the product's reason for being — the 8oz Commuter is for the kind of person who would do this kind of thing. We're not advertising the cup. We're advertising a way of moving through a city, and the cup is the device that makes the way of moving legible.

THE BENEFIT

Five reasons this works — and why we're nailing it.

i.
A product launch dressed as a cultural event A new SKU on a website is forgettable. "Buy a cup, taste the city" is shareable, gameable, and lives beyond the launch window.
ii.
The Coffee Pass = a 6-week engagement loop A one-time purchase becomes eight return visits. The pass is a physical artefact people post on Instagram unprompted. Earned reach without paid budget.
iii.
Cafés absorb the unit cost of the free coffee Each café gets new foot traffic from Sttoke buyers; Sttoke gets premium retail placement at zero shelf cost. The mechanic is mutual; the spend is shared.
iv.
The number 8 is internationally portable Eight is auspicious in Chinese culture, lucky in Thai, infinity-adjacent everywhere. The same campaign IP exports to Bangkok, Shanghai, and many more…
v.
Bangkok 8 launches 1 June The same playbook, the second city. Nine cafés, three months. See "What's Next" for the full preview.

CHAPTER THREE

What's Coming Next

Activations Preview
MAY 2026 → DEC 2026

The next two quarters converge on two signature initiatives — each designed to deepen market presence and unlock new revenue corridors: Bangkok 8 in Thailand and the Sttoke × Moomin retail partnership in Taiwan.

Q2
The Playbook Goes International · Bangkok 8
JUN → AUG
MEGA · EXPORT
★ KEY HIGHLIGHT · NEXT QUARTER

Bangkok 8

Buy a cup. Taste the city. — second edition.

The Melbourne 8 playbook exports to Bangkok — same hero SKU (8oz Commuter), same Coffee Pass mechanic, second city. Nine cafés across prime Bangkok neighbourhoods: Varinda Coffee (Phayathai), Brew Whale (Chatuchak), Double Slash (Watthana), Fika (Watthana), Nana Coffee (Watthana), Roots (Sathon), Rise (Sampanthawong), Factory (Yan Nawa), and Paga (Phaya Thai). Each one curated for design, specialty-coffee credibility, and neighbourhood influence. Launch window: 1 June to 31 August 2026.

Cafés9
Window3 months
Launch1 JUN
WhyReplication
The nine cafés. From left to right: Varinda Coffee (Phayathai), Brew Whale (Chatuchak), Double Slash (Watthana), Fika (Watthana), Nana Coffee Roasters (Watthana), Roots (Sathon), Rise Coffee (Sampanthawong), Factory Coffee (Yan Nawa), and Paga Microroastery (Phaya Thai). Each curated for design excellence, specialty-coffee credentials, and neighbourhood influence. Same selection bar as Melbourne — no compromises on venue quality.
